args.failure_reason: warning header in Title Case instead of ALL CAPS

text.text: I'll look up the controlling regulation for the warning header format.

input.failure_reason: warning header in Title Case instead of ALL CAPS

result.explanation: Not found in the regulations on file.

args.question: what is the required format for the government health warning header on an alcohol label

text.text: Let me try a regulatory lookup instead to find the exact requirement:

input.question: what is the required format for the government health warning header on an alcohol label

result.answer:
Based only on the regulations on file:

27 CFR 4.32(a) — Labels of wine must state: the brand name; the class, type, or other designation; the alcohol content; the name and address of the bottler or importer; and the net contents. The container must also bear the Government health warning statement required by 27 CFR part 16.

27 CFR 5.63(a) — Labels of distilled spirits must state: the brand name; the class and type or other designation; the alcohol content; the name and address of the bottler or importer; the net contents; and, where applicable, a commodity statement. The container must also bear the Government health warning statement required by 27 CFR part 16.

27 CFR 16.22(a) — The health warning statement must be readily legible, appear on a contrasting background, and be separate and apart from other information. The words 'GOVERNMENT WARNING' must appear in capital letters and in bold type. The remainder of the statement may not appear in bold.

transcript.text:
**The violation is in 27 CFR 16.22(a).**

The regulation explicitly requires that the words **"GOVERNMENT WARNING" must appear in capital letters** (ALL CAPS) and in bold type. If your label shows the warning header in Title Case (e.g., "Government Warning" instead of "GOVERNMENT WARNING"), it fails this requirement.

The rest of the warning statement may not appear in bold, but that header must be ALL CAPS and bold—no exceptions.
